data.title: Samsung Bioepis and Teva enter into a partnership for commercialization of EPYSQLI
data.description:
• Samsung Bioepis and Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) announced that the companies have entered into a license, development and commercialization agreement for EPYSQLI, Samsung Bioepis’ biosimilar to Soliris in the U.S..
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Samsung Bioepis will be responsible for the development, regulatory registration, manufacture and supply of the product, while Teva will be responsible for commercialization of the product in the U.S.
  • The financial terms of the agreement remain confidential.
  • This agreement is a testament to both Samsung Bioepis’ and Teva's strong track record in the field of biosimilars and the potential value that these biosimilars could deliver to patients and healthcare systems.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: TLRY, DAL, MVST, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped on Friday as investors evaluated a much hotter-than-expected December jobs report and awaited the release of Consumer Sentiment data later in the day. These key economic indicators are closely watched, as the jobs report provides insight into employment trends, while consumer sentiment offers a gauge of consumer confidence and spending patterns, both of which influence market expectations for economic growth and potential policy decisions.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Shares of Microvast Holdings (NASDAQ:MVST) surged 52% following the company's announcement of a major milestone in the development of its True All-Solid-State Battery (ASSB) technology. This breakthrough represents a significant advancement in battery safety, energy density, and efficiency, offering solutions for applications like data center backup power systems and electric school buses while also paving the way for innovations in electric vehicles and robotics. Unlike conventional lithium-ion or semi-solid-state batteries, Microvast's ASSB eliminates liquid electrolytes and features a bipolar stacking architecture, which allows higher voltages unattainable by traditional battery designs. As Microvast progresses to pilot production, the company aims to revolutionize the battery industry with scalable, customizable energy storage solutions for high-demand sectors.
  • Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares rose more than 3% after the company inked an agreement with NASA to provide Neutron launch services through its existing VADR (Venture-Class Acquisition of Dedicated and Rideshare) contract. Neutron, Rocket Lab's new medium-lift reusable rocket, will expand the company's ability to deliver a wide range of missions, including CubeSats, Class D missions, and other payloads, across various orbits. Additionally, the Neutron rocket is positioned to be integrated into the U.S. Government’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Lane 1 program, a $5.6B IDIQ contract. Neutron is scheduled to debut with its first launch from Rocket Lab's Launch Complex 3 in Virginia in mid-2025.
  • Formerly known as Smart Global Holdings, Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ:PENG) shares soared 17% after the company revealed a strategic collaboration agreement with SK Telecom and SK hynix to develop and deliver comprehensive AI data center solutions. The agreement, signed at CES 2025 in Las Vegas, follows SKT's $200M strategic investment in Penguin Solutions, which was completed in December. This follows the company's upbeat FQ1 results.
  • Shares of Delta Air Lines (DAL) rose nearly 6% after the company reported a strong FQ4 2024 performance, exceeding both top- and bottom-line expectations. The boost was attributed to a surge in demand throughout the quarter, with November and December generating four of the airline's top ten revenue days, alongside double-digit growth in cash bookings driven by both leisure and corporate travelers. Corporate sales also saw double-digit increases. International revenue saw improvement across all regions, with a 6% Y/Y growth in international passenger revenue for the quarter. Looking ahead to 2025, CEO Ed Bastian highlighted the expectation for sustained strong travel demand, with an emphasis on premium products and experiences, positioning Delta for a record financial year. The airline forecasts Q1 EPS between $0.70 and $1.00 (midpoint $0.85) vs. consensus of $0.76.

Biggest stock losers

  • Tilray Brands (TLRY) slumped over 12% following the release of mixed FQ2 results and the reaffirmation of its FY2025 revenue outlook. The company reported a 10% Y/Y decline in adjusted EBITDA for the quarter, dropping to $9M, partly due to a $1.8M impact from the beverage segment's SKU rationalization. In a bid to drive efficiency, Tilray introduced a synergy program called Project 420, aimed at achieving its $25M synergy target through portfolio optimization, operational improvements, and cost-saving initiatives.

data.title: Perma-Pipe International announces $43M contract award in the Middle East
data.description:
• Perma-Pipe International Holdings (NASDAQ:PPIH) said on Friday that it has received a formal letter of award for a development project located in the GCC region. 
  • Perma-Pipe will provide thermal insulation, anti-corrosion coatings, and other services from its Abu Dhabi facility. Project commencement is expected to begin in the third quarter of 2025. 
  • The value of this project is estimated to exceed $43 million.
  • Perma-Pipe International Holdings (NASDAQ:PPIH) shares were up 7.5% during Friday trading hours.

data.title: Silynxcom rises 6%, guides record 2024 revenue
data.description:
• Silynxcom (NYSE:SYNX) announced preliminary unaudited for the twelve months ended December 31, 2024, expects revenue estimated at $9.2 million in 2024, up 23% Y/Y.
  • Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024, totaled $3.2 million.
  • “Our continued collaboration with leading customers, including the U.S. Air Force and the Israel Defense Forces, highlights the trust placed in our innovative communication solutions. With the successful introduction of new products, expansion into new territories, and a steadfast commitment to delivering cutting-edge technology, we believe that we are well-positioned to sustain this momentum and further solidify our leadership in the defense sector.” said Nir Klein, Silynxcom’s CEO.
  • The stock price increased 6% on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: NeuroOne Medical Technologies announces higher Q1 prelim revenue
data.description:
• NeuroOne Medical Technologies (NASDAQ:NMTC) announced preliminary Q1 2025 revenue to increase to $6.2M, compared to $1M in Q1 2024.
  • Q1 2025 includes one-time $3M payment of collaboration revenue from the expanded distribution agreement with Zimmer Biomet for commercialization of the company’s OneRF Ablation System, expected to be fully recognized in Q1 2025.
  • The company expects product revenues to increase 227% to $3.2M in Q1 2025, compared to product revenue of $1M in Q1 2024.
  • For FY 2025, NMTC is reiterating guidance specific to product revenues, which exclude collaboration revenues, to range between $8M and $10M, with product gross margin to range between 47% and 51%.
  • Complete financial results for the quarter are expected to be announced in February 2025.
  • NMTC shares were down 2.8% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: AbbVie to record $3.5B impairment charge for Cerevel drug
data.description:
AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) said it expects to record an impairment charge of approximately $3.5B related to the drug emraclidine, which it acquired through its acquisition of Cerevel Therapeutics last year.

The drugmaker disclosed in November that two Phase 2 studies of the drug failed to meet their primary endpoint. The drug was being evaluated for the treatment of schizophrenia.

AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) said it will continue to evaluate information related to Cerevel's clinical development programs and will monitor the remaining intangible assets of approximately $3.6 billion, according to an SEC filing.

AbbVie (ABBV) acquired Cerevel in August for around $8.7B.

data.title: Everest Group upgraded to Outperform at BMO on 'material catalysts'
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets raised its rating recommendation on Everest Group (NYSE:EG) stock to Outperform from Market Perform on Friday as analyst Michael Zaremski reckoned a sizable reserve charge in Q4 2024, and subsequently a management change in Q1 2025, "serve as material catalysts" for the reinsurance company.

The upgrade "is fairly straight forward," Zaremski wrote in a note to clients. "And we’ll find out how right or wrong we are in a matter of weeks because most of our thesis hinges on if EG’s new/ acting CEO + Board of Directors (which is led by a Chairman who was the former CEO and owns >$100 million of stock) is bold enough to take material near-term EPS pain."

Everest (NYSE:EG) will need to take a reserve charge of $812M for Q4 2024, as well as lower forward earnings-per-share guidance by about 7%, in order for investors to gain more confidence in owning the stock, the analyst said.

So, with the stock underperforming peers by more than 25% on a trailing-twelve-month basis, Zaremski estimated the weakness "gives EG’s CEO+Board more willingness to reset the decks lower in both of its operating segments (not just in its primary insurance segment), which we think investors will ultimately applaud over the course of 2025, so long as EG can indeed start printing healthy EPS results come 2025 earnings."

BMO's Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

(EG) shares slipped 0.5% in Friday premarket trading.

In November, Yaron Kinar of Jefferies had downgraded Everest (EG) to Hold from Buy due to concerns around the reserve increase.

data.title: Goosehead Insurance subsidiary gets new $300M term loan
data.description:
• Goosehead Insurance (NASDAQ:GSHD) said that its subsidiary, Goosehead Insurance Holdings closed on a $300 million term loan B and $75 million revolving credit facility.
  • The term loan facility will bear interest at a rate of SOFR plus 3.50%.
  • Proceeds from the new term loan agreement will be used to retire the company’s existing $93 million term loan, pay a one-time special dividend, and other general corporate purposes.
  • The maturation date of the new term loan facility is January 8, 2032.

data.title: Ivanhoe Mines launches offering of $600 million senior notes
data.description:
• Ivanhoe Mines (TSX:IVN:CA) on Friday said it has commenced an offering (the "Offering") of $600 million senior notes due 2030.
  • In connection with the offering, the company entered into a $120.0 million revolving credit facility agreement dated December 22, 2024, with, amongst others, the guarantors and the lenders named therein.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering of the Notes will be used for general corporate purposes and to pay certain fees and expenses relating to the offering, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fresh Vine Wine reports non-compliance with NYSE American
data.description:
• Fresh Vine Wine (NYSE:VINE) received a notice from NYSE American stating that it is not in compliance because it missed holding its annual meeting for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, by the December 31, 2024 deadline.
  • Fresh Vine intends to hold a meeting of stockholders in the next several months with respect to the proposed business combination with Adifex Holdings, expecting it will bring VINE into NYSE American compliance.
  • Source: Press release
